methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Valinor Enterprises is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Trae Stephens and Grant Verstandig that has raised $85M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $85M total raised as a baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $170M. The peer-median method benchmarks against four same-stage, same-sector comparables with a median valuation of $225M (range: $125M–$500M), anchoring directly to observable market pricing in defense-tech.

These methods converge on a valuation range of $144M to $259M, with a median implied value of $198M. The $55M spread between the stage-progression result ($170M) and the peer median ($225M) reflects heterogeneity in the comparable set—notably the wide $375M gap between highest and lowest peer valuations. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, contract pipeline specificity, and whether Valinor's technology differentiation supports positioning at the higher end of the peer range. The median $198M estimate assumes baseline competitive positioning within the Defense & Security frontier-tech cohort.
